title: System Failure
description: A primary subsystem has failed mid-voyage. Engineering wants parts and power; the council wants a plan.

outcomes.label: Emergency repair
outcomes.description: Spend minerals and credits to restore the system fully.
outcomes.log: Engineering crews restored the failed system at material cost.

outcomes.label: Reroute power
outcomes.description: Bypass the failure. Cheap now, a debt for later.
outcomes.log: Power was rerouted around the failure. The bypass will outlive its welcome.

title: Population Growth
description: A healthy cohort of births strains food planning but promises a stronger next generation.

outcomes.label: Expand rations
outcomes.description: Feed the boom.
outcomes.log: The ship's population grew, and the galley budget grew with it.

outcomes.label: Hold rations steady
outcomes.description: No new allocations. The nursery decks will remember.
outcomes.log: Rations held steady through the birth cohort. Some decks kept score.

title: Arrival Burn
description: The destination system resolves from a bright dot into a place. Final approach requires a heavy deceleration burn.

outcomes.label: Textbook burn
outcomes.description: By the book, on the fuel budget.
outcomes.log: The arrival burn went by the book. The observation decks were crowded for a week.

title: Cultural Schism
description: Two decks now keep different calendars. The disagreement has stopped being polite.

outcomes.label: Council mediation
outcomes.description: Spend influence to reconcile the factions.
outcomes.log: The council brokered a settlement between the divided decks.

outcomes.label: Let it run its course
outcomes.description: Cultures drift. Intervening has costs too.
outcomes.log: The schism was left to run its course. The ship is quieter, and colder.

title: Micrometeoroid Storm
description: A debris front the forward sensors caught too late. The hull is taking hits and the shelters are filling.

outcomes.label: Brace and seal
outcomes.description: Shutter the outer decks and ride it out. Slow, safe, hard on nerves.
outcomes.log: The ship shuttered its outer decks and rode the storm out, scarred but whole.

outcomes.label: Burn around it
outcomes.description: Spend fuel to steer clear. The schedule slips; the hull survives.
outcomes.log: A hard burn carried the ship clear of the debris front, at the cost of the fuel budget.

title: Coolant Breach
description: A coolant line has ruptured in the reactor annex. Every minute of delay is a degree the core will not forgive.

outcomes.label: Vent the annex
outcomes.description: Blow the section to space. The core is saved; the annex crew are not all accounted for.
outcomes.log: The annex was vented to save the core. The memorial wall grew by a column.

outcomes.label: Order a manual patch
outcomes.description: Send engineers in with what they have. Expensive, and not without risk.
outcomes.log: Engineers patched the breach by hand. The reactor runs, and runs hot.

title: The Skills Drought
description: The last founding specialists are retiring faster than apprentices can replace them. Institutional memory is thinning.

outcomes.label: Crash apprenticeship
outcomes.description: Pull hands from other duties to train replacements now.
outcomes.log: A crash apprenticeship filled the benches, if not yet the shoes.

outcomes.label: Lean on the records
outcomes.description: Trust the archives to carry what the elders knew. Some of it will not translate.
outcomes.log: The crews leaned on the archives. Something in the doing, not the knowing, was lost.

title: The Restless Generation
description: A cohort born to the corridors has decided the mission is their grandparents' promise, not theirs.

outcomes.label: Seat them on the council
outcomes.description: Fold the dissent into the institution. It changes both.
outcomes.log: The restless generation was given a seat and a stake. The council sounds younger now.

outcomes.label: Hold the founders' line
outcomes.description: The charter is not up for renegotiation. Say so plainly.
outcomes.log: The council held the founders' line. The corridors went quiet in a way that keeps.

title: The Halfway Beacon
description: The ship passes the midpoint marker its founders laid in the plan. Half the voyage is behind; no one living chose the far half.

outcomes.label: Mark the crossing
outcomes.description: A day of ceremony and rest. Morale answers.
outcomes.log: The ship marked the halfway crossing. For a day, the corridors felt like a procession.

title: A Silent Hull
description: Sensors snag a derelict on the transit lane — an older ship, dark, drifting. It answers no hail.

outcomes.label: Board and salvage
outcomes.description: Take what the dead ship offers. Not everyone approves.
outcomes.log: The boarding parties stripped the derelict of what it could spare. The holds are fuller; the mess halls quieter.

outcomes.label: Log it and pass
outcomes.description: Record the position, say the words, keep the schedule.
outcomes.log: The ship logged the derelict's position, spoke for its dead, and passed on.

title: The Founders' Creed
description: A movement wants the founding charter read aloud each year, word for word, forever. Another wants it revised for a ship the founders never saw.

outcomes.label: Enshrine the charter
outcomes.description: The founders' words become law and liturgy both.
outcomes.log: The charter was enshrined, unaltered. The ship knows exactly who it was meant to be.

outcomes.label: Amend the charter
outcomes.description: A living document for a living ship. Every word changed is a small funeral.
outcomes.log: The charter was amended. The founders would not recognize the ship; the ship is not sure it minds.

title: The Right to Name
description: The destination needs a name for the charts and the histories. Every faction has one ready, and a reason it must win.

outcomes.label: Honor the founders' name
outcomes.description: The name they wrote into the plan, kept.
outcomes.log: The destination kept the name its founders chose. Some traditions are only words until you keep them.

outcomes.label: Let the ship choose
outcomes.description: A vote, deck by deck. Messy, and theirs.
outcomes.log: The ship named its own destination by vote. The charts bear a word no founder wrote.

title: The Slow Crack
description: A hairline fracture is walking its way across a spine frame. It is not urgent yet. 'Yet' is doing a great deal of work in that sentence.

outcomes.label: Reinforce it now
outcomes.description: Pull minerals and crews to brace the frame before it spreads.
outcomes.log: The spine frame was braced before the crack could vote on the matter.

outcomes.label: Monitor and defer
outcomes.description: Sensor it, log it, spend nothing. A problem for a later council.
outcomes.log: The crack was catalogued and left to grow. It will keep its appointment.

title: Recycled Air
description: Something is moving through the ventilation the way rumors move through the decks. The medics want the decks sealed; the foremen want them working.

outcomes.label: Seal the decks
outcomes.description: Lose the labor, keep the lungs.
outcomes.log: The decks were sealed and the outbreak starved of hosts. Production limped; the ward filled slowly.

outcomes.label: Work through it
outcomes.description: Keep the ship moving. The strong will carry the weak, or not.
outcomes.log: The ship worked through the sickness. The memorial wall did the accounting.

title: The Thin Cohort
description: This generation came small. The nurseries have empty cribs and the actuaries have furrowed brows.

outcomes.label: Incentivize births
outcomes.description: Spend influence and rations to fill the cribs.
outcomes.log: The council's incentives filled the nurseries. The next census will thank them.

outcomes.label: Accept a lean generation
outcomes.description: A smaller crew eats less and argues less. It also builds less.
outcomes.log: The lean cohort grew up close-knit and few. The ship ran quieter, and shorter-handed.

title: The Crowded Classroom
description: The school decks were built for a smaller ship. The waiting lists are longer than the curriculum.

outcomes.label: Expand the schools
outcomes.description: Convert deck space and credits into classrooms.
outcomes.log: New classrooms opened on a converted cargo deck. A generation will be better taught for it.

title: The Fork in the Dark
description: Two viable courses to the destination diverge here: a fast lane through a debris shelf, or a slow safe arc. The nav computer wants a decision it cannot make.

outcomes.label: Take the fast lane
outcomes.description: Shave years off the crossing. Bet the hull on good sensors.
outcomes.log: The ship threaded the debris shelf and gained a decade. The hull wears the toll.

outcomes.label: Take the safe arc
outcomes.description: Add years, keep the plating.
outcomes.log: The ship took the long safe arc. The schedule slipped; nothing else did.

title: A Voice on the Relay
description: An automated relay, older than the mission, is broadcasting a handshake into the dark. It wants to talk. It may only be an echo.

outcomes.label: Answer the relay
outcomes.description: Trade data with the old machine. Knowledge, and exposure.
outcomes.log: The ship answered the relay and came away with charts and a strange new vocabulary.

outcomes.label: Run silent
outcomes.description: Log its position, tell it nothing, keep the ship's shape a secret.
outcomes.log: The ship passed the relay in silence. Whatever it wanted, it will keep wanting.

title: The First Rite
description: A generation born entirely in transit must decide how to bury its dead — the founders' soil rites, impossible here, or something the ship invents for itself.

outcomes.label: Keep the soil rite
outcomes.description: Use precious hydroponic loam for symbolic burial. Costly, and rooted.
outcomes.log: The dead were laid in borrowed soil, as the founders would know. The farms felt the loss.

outcomes.label: Invent a ship rite
outcomes.description: Commit the dead to the void with new words. Cheaper, and untethered.
outcomes.log: The ship spoke new words over its first spacers' dead. A tradition began that no founder wrote.

title: The Last Founders
description: The final three crew who were awake at departure petition the council: they want the mission's original wording read into the record before they die, unamended.

outcomes.label: Honor the petition
outcomes.description: Read the founding words as written. Let the old crew hear them once more.
outcomes.log: The founding words were read whole to the last who remembered them. Some wept; none objected.

outcomes.label: Read the current charter
outcomes.description: The living ship's amended wording is the true text now. Honesty over sentiment.
outcomes.log: The amended charter was read instead. The last founders heard a mission they did not entirely recognize.

title: Reactor Scram
description: The core dropped its rods on an automatic fault. The ship runs on batteries now, and everyone can hear the silence where the hum should be.

outcomes.label: Cold restart by the book
outcomes.description: Slow, safe, hours in the dark.
outcomes.log: The core came back on a careful cold restart. The dark was long and the coffee ran out.

outcomes.label: Override and hot-restart
outcomes.description: Fast, and the fault might still be there.
outcomes.log: The engineers hot-restarted the core over its own objections. It runs; it grumbles.

title: The Tail
description: A ship with no transponder has matched the voyage's heading for three days. It is not closing. It is not leaving.

outcomes.label: Warn it off
outcomes.description: Light it up and make the ship look expensive to attack.
outcomes.log: A show of teeth sent the shadow drifting back into the dark. Probably.

outcomes.label: Hold course, say nothing
outcomes.description: Give it nothing to react to.
outcomes.log: The ship held course and the tail eventually fell away. The observation decks watched the whole time.

title: The Thickening Air
description: Two CO2 scrubbers failed in the same week. The air is going stale deck by deck and the spares are a generation old.

outcomes.label: Cannibalize for parts
outcomes.description: Strip other systems to rebuild the scrubbers.
outcomes.log: The scrubbers breathe again, assembled from the corpses of lesser machines.

outcomes.label: Ration activity
outcomes.description: Slow the ship to slow the breathing.
outcomes.log: The ship moved at half-effort until the air cleared. Everyone learned to sit very still.

title: The Idle Hands
description: Automation has outrun the population. A generation has grown up with too little to do and too much time to wonder why they are here.

outcomes.label: Fund make-work projects
outcomes.description: Spend credits on purpose, even invented purpose.
outcomes.log: The council funded a hundred small projects. Idle hands found rails to paint and songs to write.

outcomes.label: Let them find their own purpose
outcomes.description: Trust boredom to breed either art or trouble.
outcomes.log: Left to themselves, the idle generation invented new things to be, some of them useful.

title: Too Many Hands
description: This cohort trained hard for posts that are already filled. The waiting list for real work is longer than a career.

outcomes.label: Rotate the posts
outcomes.description: Share the work thin so everyone gets some.
outcomes.log: The posts were rotated so the trained could stay sharp. Nobody is idle; nobody is expert.

title: The Slingshot
description: A massive body sits just off the course. A gravity assist could bank years of fuel — if the approach is flawless.

outcomes.label: Thread the slingshot
outcomes.description: Fly close, gain speed, risk the tides.
outcomes.log: The ship whipped past the giant and came out faster and lighter on fuel. The tides only rattled the crockery.

outcomes.label: Skip the maneuver
outcomes.description: Burn the fuel, keep the plating.
outcomes.log: The council skipped the slingshot and paid in fuel for the peace of mind.

title: The Dark Stretch
description: Ahead lies a region the founders' charts simply stop describing — a long stretch where the sensors will have little to say.

outcomes.label: Run it steady
outcomes.description: Trust the plan and the dead reckoning.
outcomes.log: The ship crossed the dark stretch on dead reckoning and old faith. It came out where the charts said it would.

title: The Cache
description: A supply cache the mission planners seeded decades ago drifts on the course ahead, if its beacon can be trusted.

outcomes.label: Match and claim it
outcomes.description: Spend fuel to rendezvous with the drop.
outcomes.log: The cache was claimed on schedule, exactly as a founder a lifetime dead had promised.

outcomes.label: Pass it by
outcomes.description: Stay on the fuel budget; the holds are full enough.
outcomes.log: The cache drifted past, unopened. The ship needs nothing yet, and the founders keep their gift.

title: Born to the Corridors
description: For the first time, the dynasty's leader was born, raised, and will die aboard — with no memory, even inherited, of a world with a sky.

outcomes.label: Mark the moment
outcomes.description: Acknowledge that the ship now leads itself, from itself.
outcomes.log: The ship marked its first wholly ship-born leader. Something ended quietly; something began louder.

title: The Memory Wall
description: The memorial wall has run out of room. The council must decide whether to extend it, archive the oldest names, or begin again.

outcomes.label: Extend the wall
outcomes.description: Give the dead more room, whatever it costs the living decks.
outcomes.log: The memorial wall grew a new wing. No name will be moved while the ship still flies.

outcomes.label: Archive the oldest names
outcomes.description: Move the earliest dead into the record to make room.
outcomes.log: The oldest names went to the archive to make room for the newly gone. The founders moved over, a little.

title: The Snare
description: The ship has run into a lattice of old orbital debris — cables, panels, the bones of something that failed here. It is fouling the forward sensors and worse.

outcomes.label: Cut free
outcomes.description: Send crews out to clear it by hand. Slow, and exposed.
outcomes.log: The debris lattice was cut away over three shifts of dangerous work. The forward array sees again.

outcomes.label: Power through
outcomes.description: Push forward and let the hull take what it takes.
outcomes.log: The ship shouldered through the snare and came out the other side scored and free.

title: The Cascade
description: One tripped breaker became ten. Half the ship is dark and the load-balancing computer is asking the council a question it was built to answer itself.

outcomes.label: Shed the load
outcomes.description: Cut non-essential decks to save the core grid.
outcomes.log: Whole decks went dark to save the grid. People learned which of their comforts were negotiable.

outcomes.label: Rebalance by hand
outcomes.description: Spend parts and hours coaxing the grid back whole.
outcomes.log: Engineers rebalanced the grid by hand, deck by deck, until the lights came back on together.

title: The Prodigy
description: A child in the new cohort reads the ship's systems the way others read faces. The council can fast-track them, or let them grow up ordinary.

outcomes.label: Fast-track the prodigy
outcomes.description: Give them every system and every hour.
outcomes.log: The prodigy was given the run of the ship's systems. In a decade the ship will be glad; the cohort is less sure now.

outcomes.label: Let them be a child
outcomes.description: No cohort should carry the ship on one set of shoulders.
outcomes.log: The council let the prodigy grow up ordinary. The ship kept its gift in reserve, and its childhood intact.

title: The Debt Comes Due
description: The maintenance the founders deferred and the last three generations ignored has all come due in the same decade. Something must be chosen, and something let go.

outcomes.label: Fund a full overhaul
outcomes.description: Pay the whole debt at once. It hurts now or later.
outcomes.log: The ship paid its inherited maintenance debt in full. The treasury is lighter; the ship will outlast the council that paid.

outcomes.label: Patch what fails
outcomes.description: Fix only what breaks. Cheaper, and a wager.
outcomes.log: The ship patched only what failed and left the rest to the next generation, as had every generation before.

title: First Light
description: For the first time, the destination star can be picked out with the naked eye from the observation deck — a specific point of light, not a coordinate.

outcomes.label: Gather and look
outcomes.description: Call the ship to the windows.
outcomes.log: The ship crowded the observation decks to see its destination as a star. Some had waited their whole lives to look.

title: The Final Approach
description: The founders wrote a final-approach plan for a ship they never saw finished. The council can ratify it as written or amend it with everything the voyage has learned.

outcomes.label: Ratify as written
outcomes.description: Trust the plan the founders sealed.
outcomes.log: The founders' approach plan was ratified unchanged. The ship will arrive exactly as it was told to.

outcomes.label: Amend with what we know
outcomes.description: The voyage taught things the founders could not know.
outcomes.log: The approach plan was amended with a lifetime of hard-won data. The founders would not recognize it; it is better.

title: A Voice From Home
description: A tight-beam message from the origin world arrives, decades stale — routine, official, and addressed to a ship the senders assume is still the one that departed.

outcomes.label: Answer home
outcomes.description: Send a reply into a fifty-year silence.
outcomes.log: The ship answered the old signal from home. The reply will arrive to a world that may no longer be listening.

outcomes.label: Log it and move on
outcomes.description: Home is a place none of them will see. Face forward.
outcomes.log: The message from home was logged and archived, unanswered. The ship's home is the ship now.

title: The Last Sky
description: The last surviving photograph of an open planetary sky — the founders' sky — has degraded to almost nothing. The archivists ask whether to restore it, or let it finish fading.

outcomes.label: Restore the image
outcomes.description: Spend to keep the founders' sky.
outcomes.log: The last sky was painstakingly restored. A ship born to corridors keeps one window onto the world it left.

outcomes.label: Let it fade
outcomes.description: Some things are meant to be let go.
outcomes.log: The last photograph of an open sky was allowed to finish fading. The ship's children will never miss what they cannot picture.

title: The Freeze
description: A coolant line froze and split where the ship runs coldest. If the crack spreads to the main loop, a whole section goes dark.

outcomes.label: Bleed the line
outcomes.description: Purge and reroute before it spreads. Costs power and hours.
outcomes.log: The frozen line was bled and rerouted before the crack could reach the main loop. Cold work, cleanly done.

outcomes.label: Seal the section
outcomes.description: Close it off and lose the deck rather than risk the loop.
outcomes.log: The frozen section was sealed off and abandoned. The main loop is safe; the ship is one deck smaller.

title: The Bloom
description: Something is growing in the water reclamation tanks that the ship's biology was not designed to host. It is spreading faster than the filters can name it.

outcomes.label: Sterilize the tanks
outcomes.description: Flush and burn the system clean. Wasteful, and thorough.
outcomes.log: The reclamation tanks were sterilized to bare metal. The bloom is gone, and so is a season's margin of water and food.

outcomes.label: Quarantine and study
outcomes.description: Isolate it and hope the filters catch up. Cheaper, and a gamble.
outcomes.log: The bloom was quarantined and studied rather than burned out. It is contained, for now, and the crew drink boiled water and worry.

title: The Slowdown
description: The maintenance cohort has stopped working to the letter of a contract the founders wrote for a different ship. Nothing is broken yet. Everything is slower.

outcomes.label: Concede the terms
outcomes.description: Rewrite the cohort's terms in their favor.
outcomes.log: The council conceded the cohort's terms. The work resumed at full speed, and every cohort now knows the council can be moved.

outcomes.label: Hold the line
outcomes.description: The contract stands. Wait them out.
outcomes.log: The council held the founders' terms. The slowdown ended in sullen compliance, and something between the decks did not fully mend.

title: The Berth Lottery
description: This generation was born larger than the last, and there are more people than private berths. Someone decides who sleeps in the open decks.

outcomes.label: Expand the quarters
outcomes.description: Convert cargo and hold space into homes.
outcomes.log: Cargo space was cut open and rebuilt into berths. The ship carries less and shelters more.

outcomes.label: Run the lottery
outcomes.description: Assign the open decks by lot. Fair, and resented.
outcomes.log: Private berths were assigned by lottery. The ship called it fair, and the losers of the draw learned to sleep in the light.

title: The Scout Returns
description: A probe the founders flung ahead a lifetime ago has finally answered — a thin stream of data about the place the ship is aimed at. Some of it does not match the charts the voyage was planned around.

outcomes.label: Trust the new data
outcomes.description: Revise the plan around what the probe actually found.
outcomes.log: The probe's data was trusted over the founders' charts, and the approach was quietly rebuilt around a world no one had promised.

outcomes.label: Keep the founders' charts
outcomes.description: One probe is one probe. Trust the plan that got you here.
outcomes.log: The council kept faith with the founders' charts. The probe's warnings were filed, and the ship held the course it was given.

title: The Midpoint Census
description: The ship takes a full census at the mathematical midpoint of the voyage — every name aboard, living and remembered, counted once against the founders' manifest.

outcomes.label: Count them all
outcomes.description: Read the whole manifest aloud.
outcomes.log: The midpoint census was read aloud over three days, every name from the founders' manifest to the newest birth. The ship felt, briefly, like one thing.

title: The Founder's Ledger
description: A founder's private ledger surfaces in the archives — not the official record, but the doubts, the debts, and the compromises behind the mission's clean origin story.

outcomes.label: Publish the ledger
outcomes.description: Let the ship see its founders whole.
outcomes.log: The founder's private ledger was published entire. The founders are smaller now, and truer, and harder to worship.

outcomes.label: Seal the ledger
outcomes.description: Some myths hold a ship together. Let this one stand.
outcomes.log: The ledger was sealed back into the archive. The founders keep their clean story, and the ship keeps the myth it steers by.

title: The Renaming
description: A movement has grown to rename the ship — not for the world it left, which no one aboard remembers, but for the corridors that are the only home its people have known.

outcomes.label: Rename the ship
outcomes.description: Let the ship be named by those who live in it.
outcomes.log: The ship was renamed by the generation that lives in it, over the objections of the archive. The old name is a footnote now, and the corridors answer to their own.

outcomes.label: Keep the founders' name
outcomes.description: The name is a promise to a place. Keep it.
outcomes.log: The founders' name was kept. The ship still carries the word for a world none of its people will ever walk, because the founders asked it to.
